entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Multi-approver workflo...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Multi-Approver Workflow for Supplier Invoices by Cost Center in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

To implement a multi-approver workflow for supplier invoices based on cost centers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can follow these steps:

1. Standard Mechanism for Multi-Approver Setup

Currently, the standard mechanism in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not directly support assigning multiple approvers based on cost centers for supplier invoices. The ‘Responsible Person’ field in cost center master data only allows a single person to be assigned.

2. Using ‘Manage Teams and Responsibilities’ App

The ‘Manage Teams and Responsibilities’ app can be used to define teams and assign functions to team members,  but it does not directly map to supplier invoice approval against a cost center. This app is typically used for defining roles and responsibilities in other contexts, such as journal entry verification.

3. Workflow Configuration for Supplier Invoices

You can configure flexible workflows for supplier invoices using the ‘Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices’ app.  This app allows you to define condition-based workflows for approval processes, including multi-step approvals. However, the configuration for cost center-based approval is not directly supported.

4. Guidance and Documentation

For more detailed guidance, you can refer to the SAP Help Portal and community blogs that discuss flexible workflows and their configurations. These resources can provide insights into how to set up workflows for supplier invoices, although they may not cover cost center-specific scenarios directly.

If you need a custom solution, you might consider using extensibility options or consulting with SAP experts to explore potential customizations or workarounds.

Conclusion

While the standard S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not directly support multi-approver workflows for supplier invoices based on cost centers, you can explore flexible workflows and team configurations for other types of approvals. For specific requirements, custom solutions may be necessary.

For more details, see the following information:

A Configuration Example of How to Optimize Journal Entry Verification

Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: credit note and Debit note
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Round‑off Calculation for Credit Note and Debit Note Documents

  • General behaviour

    • Rounding is a configuration option that applies to the total amount (including VAT/GST) of an invoicing document, whether it is an invoice, a credit memo (credit note) or a debit memo (debit note).
    • When the rounding function is active, the rounded total appears in its own line on the document, and the rounding difference is automatically distributed to the individual line items.
    • The same rounding key and rules are used for both debit‑type and credit‑type amounts; the configuration defines separate “Debit Rounding Unit” and “Credit Rounding Unit” values, but in the examples provided they are set to the same value.

  • Rounding rules by currency (as defined in the “Rounding Rules” view)

    Currency Debit Rounding Unit Credit Rounding Unit Amount Method (both) Example of rounding
    Swedish Krona (SEK)1.001.00“Round up or down to the nearest whole number.”138,22 SEK → 138,00 SEK; 138,68 SEK → 139,00 SEK; 138,50 SEK → 139,00 SEK
    Singapore Dollar (SGD)1.001.00“Round to the nearest 5 cent.”138.22 SGD → 138.20 SGD; 138.68 SGD → 138.70 SGD
    Swiss Franc (CHF)0.050.05“Round to the nearest whole number” (applied to 0.05‑CHF increments).582.02 CHF → 582.00 CHF; 582.03 CHF → 582.05 CHF

  • Key configuration steps (common to all currencies)

    1. Open Define Key for Invoice Rounding (ID 104810).
    2. In the Rounding Key view, confirm the C (Convergent FI‑CA) application area and enter the rounding key description.
    3. On the Rounding Key tab, specify transaction data for rounding carry‑forward and write‑off.
    4. In the Rounding Rules view, enter the relevant currency and set the debit/credit rounding units and methods as shown above.
    5. Save the entries.
    6. Assign the rounding key to the appropriate invoicing process via Assign Key for Invoice Rounding (ID 104811).

  • What the context does not specify

    • The documents do not provide a separate, detailed example for a debit note (debit memo) rounding calculation; they only illustrate rounding for invoices and credit memos.
    • Therefore, the exact rounding outcome for a debit note would follow the same unit and method defined for the “Debit Rounding Unit” in the relevant currency configuration.

The above information is derived directly from the provided configuration documentation for rounding in Convergent Invoicing.

In case this information doesn't help you resolve your issue, please let us know.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Dear @KhaingZinTun,

You're not limited to static fields, what matters is where the logic runs:

  • Frontend logic / custom actions (controller extensions): not available the app's synchronous views disable them in the adaptation project
  • Backend logic to populate field data: supported, via the app's released BAdIs

For your case, pulling Product Master Data into new fields should be handled through the Product Details Enhancement BAdI RETAIL_ST_PL_PRODUCT_ENH. This BAdI enriches the product data in the backend, and a UI field can bind to that data only if the data is exposed in the app’s data model/OData response

More details below:

  • SAP Help – Extensibility: Look Up Retail Products
  • SAP Help – Controller Extensions

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Reset of CBC configuration in customizing tenant necessary (for CBC Workspace "DEV - Customizing")
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

we are currently in the early Explore phase of our SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation project and have just started configuration activities in Central Business Configuration (CBC) for our Development System respectively Customizing tenant.

During the initial scoping and deployment, we unfortunately missed including the scope items:
- 5HG
- 5KF

Based on the official “Availability Dependencies” documentation, these scope items cannot be added afterwards to an already deployed CBC workspace.

Since we are still at a very early stage (no productive configuration or dependent activities completed yet), we kindly request your support with one of the following options:

1. A full CBC Reset of our workspace  

   Affected systems / URLs:
   - Frontend (S/4HANA):  
        - CBC Backend:  
     OR

2. If a reset is not recommended or technically feasible, please advise on the best alternative approach, such as:
   - Re-provisioning of the CBC workspace / Customizing tenant (component:  XX‑S4C‑OPR‑SRV)
   - Creating a new workspace with correct initial scoping
   - Any SAP-recommended procedure to include the missing scope items

Our main objective is to ensure a clean and correct project setup before proceeding further in the Explore phase.

As we are still at a very early stage, data loss or reinitialization is fully acceptable.

We appreciate your guidance on the most efficient and recommended way forward.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Reset of CBC configuration in customizing tenant nece...
entry.content:
Hi!

Thank you for your question.

As we understand, you identified that scope items 5HG and 5KF were not selected during the initial scoping activities in your SAP Central Business Configuration workspace.

The good news is that, if your workspace is still in the early implementation stages and the deployment has not yet been executed via Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity in CBC, there is no need for a tenant reset. In this situation, the recommended approach is to restart the setup process by deleting the workspace and perform the initial scoping again, ensuring that all required scope items are included from the beginning.

This is generally the most efficient path forward, as it allows you to establish the correct project scope before any downstream configuration activities, deployments, or dependencies are introduced and helps avoid potential complications later in the implementation.

As described in KBA 3377871 - Not possible to activate scope items 5HG and 5KF in SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me, when deployment has not yet occurred, recreating the setup with the correct scope selection is the preferred approach. Once the required scope items have been included, you can proceed with the project activities as planned.

This is mentioned in "If the scope items 5HG and 5KF were not activated during the initial scoping phase and no content was deployed to the target system via activity Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure, the Workspace can be deleted and recreated from scratch. For more details, refer to SAP Help Portal SAP Help - Deleting a Workspace."

We hope this clarifies the available options and helps you move forward with confidence.

Best Regards!

entry.content: In addition to what others have said (i.e., basically yes, you can have some custom developments) - for details on what is permitted, I would like to refer you to the scope, implementation, and service catalog documents in the ECP product page (SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal).  Basically, customizations should not interfere with Support Package implementation and should be related to the payroll use-case.  What you really need to avoid is direct modifications on SAP standard objects.

entry.title: Re: Exclude non-working hours in Avg Cycle time in Signav...
entry.content:
Hi @manuelmeindl22 ,

Can you elaborate on this answer please? How would importing a factory calendar help with the cycle time calculation? 

For example, assume we have a holiday on January 1st, Event A occurring on December 31st, and Event B occurring on January 2nd. A standard cycle time calculation would yield 2 days. Incorporating our factory calendar, our desired cycle time is 1 day by excluding the January 1st holiday. In either case, the Signavio eventlog is the same:

 

c_caseidc_eventnamec_time
123Event ADecember 31st
123Event BJanuary 2nd

so how does incorporating a factory calendar help calculate the desired cycle time between the events?

An option would be to put all event collector code in one event collector to be able to create the entire eventlog in one script then do a lead/lag comparison of rows and then find the difference between subsequent events while excluding certain dates. However, this is not practical.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Project Cost Line Items – Loss of Cumulative and Commitment-Based Measures in Recent Releases
entry.content:
When raising this concern through official product support channels, the standard response (KBA 3742776 - APPS F6991 and F6992 do not display Commitments) states that the new applications are working as designed.

The recommended workaround provided by Product Support is to steer users back to the classical SAP GUI Report Writer/Painter transactions:

  • S_ALR_8701354 (Project Info System: Costs/Revenues/Expenditures/Funds)
  • S_ALR_87013542 (Actual/Commitment/Total/Plan in Project)

As SAP specialists striving to implement SAP Activate best practices and driving Clean Core governance, this workaround presents an architectural contradiction. We are pushing organizations to adopt the modern Fiori UX and deprecate legacy GUI reporting, yet the standard digital core forces a regression to 20-year-old ABAP report engines to perform core financial cross-module validations (PS-FI-CO-MM).

entry.title: Recurring deductions not editable in ONB2.0 hiring process
entry.content:
Hello community, 

Would like to invite everyone to vote for the following influence item that will benefit our Onboarding module users and end users:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/377027 

Sharing the brief here and please help us with your vote:

The current limitation prevents business teams from maintaining recurring allowances and deductions during the onboarding stage, which leads to incomplete compensation data at the time of hire and creates risks for downstream processes such as Payroll. It also introduces inefficiencies by requiring manual updates after employee creation and results in an inconsistent user experience, as this is the only Compensation portlet that does not function during onboarding. Enabling this capability earlier in the process would ensure complete and accurate data capture, reduce duplicate effort, improve data integrity, and streamline the overall hiring and payroll workflow for all stakeholders involved
 
#Onboarding2.0 ##ONB  #Onboarding #recruitment

entry.title: Re: Business Data Architecture: Defining Business Meaning
entry.content:
Hi Kristian,

 
I’ve seen similar behavior, especially from HANA calculation views.
 
From my experience, it really depends on the type of metric definition. In most cases, when I design metrics, I am mainly applying filters and relatively simple business rules on already harmonized data. In those scenarios, I haven’t really seen any major performance issues from defining the logic in views.
 
Where it can become different is if you have more complex calculations - for example metrics depending on multiple sources or layers of metrics built on top of each other. In those cases, I would typically persist the data on aggregated grain to avoid recalculating everything repeatedly. But it’s worth noting that this kind of complexity will usually impact performance regardless of whether it’s implemented in views or directly in analytical models.
 
That said, I still think it’s the better approach to define metrics in views rather than pushing them into analytical models. Even if there’s a small performance impact in some scenarios, the benefit is that you define the metric once and expose it through a reusable data product.
 
That makes it possible to reuse the same definition across multiple data products and analytical models without redefining it each time. In contrast, if the logic sits in analytical models, you quickly end up duplicating the same metric in multiple places.
 
So for me, it’s really about balance: default to defining metrics in a reusable data product, and only optimize or persist when the complexity actually justifies it.
 
Best regards
 
Allan Hald

entry.title: Understanding field(suppress) in SAP RAP and How It Differs from @UI.hidden and @Consumption.hidden
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

When working with SAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model), the Behavior Definition (BDEF) offers several keywords to control how data is exposed and handled. One of the less-discussed but powerful ones is field(suppress).

This blog is not just about syntax — it’s about what actually happens at runtime, the edge cases I hit, and why I chose suppression over readonly.

Requirement: In had one requirement, customer service reps needed to update Priority, Deadline Date, Cancel Reason, but they should never touch Created Time or Created User. These audit fields must remain intact, even during draft saves or mass updates.

What I Tried First
@UI.hidden: Hid the fields in Fiori, but they were still exposed in OData payloads.

field(readonly): Made the fields immutable, but they still appeared in EML structures — meaning a payload could attempt to overwrite them.

Both approaches failed to fully protect the audit fields.

What is field(suppress)?
field(suppress) is a static feature control in the BDEF that removes a field from RAP's transactional behavior layer. When a field is marked as suppressed, RAP excludes it from:

  • EML (Entity Manipulation Language) structures — such as TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE, TYPE STRUCTURE FOR UPDATE
  • RAP-generated behavior APIs
  • Behavior-related metadata

Runtime Behavior of Suppressed Fields
When I switched to field(suppress), here’s what I observed:

In EML statements, suppressed fields simply don’t exist. If you try to reference them, RAP silently ignores them, no runtime dump, no short dump.

If you mistakenly add them in the mapping section, RAP throws a framework error at activation time.

In draft handling, suppressed fields are still maintained internally, but you can’t overwrite them. This was critical for audit consistency.

In determinations, you cannot reference suppressed fields — the compiler blocks it. I observed this when trying to auto-populate Created By and Created At.

Syntax:

define behavior for ZI_REQHDR {
    field ( suppress ) CreatedAt, CreatedBy; 
}

Attempting to map them in the behavior pool raises:
Field "CreatedBy" cannot be suppressed.

Key insight: field(suppress) is a behavior-layer protection tool — it removes a field from RAP-generated transactional types and APIs. It is not a UI visibility control or an OData filter

Why Suppress Instead of Readonly
Readonly: Field is visible but immutable — still exposed in payloads.

Suppress: Field is completely removed from RAP’s transactional model — safest option for audit fields.

Note: Key fields (primary key fields) cannot be suppressed.

  • Suppressed fields must also be excluded from the field mapping. Attempting to include them raises a framework error:
mapping for zdt_vd_reqhdr corresponding
{
    RequestUuid   = request_uuid;
    Status        = status;
    Priority      = priority;
    ExternalId    = external_id;
    DeadlineDate  = deadline_date;
    RequesterId   = requester_id;
    CancelReason  = cancel_reason;

* // If i try to add the below fields error will get raised
*   CreatedBy     = created_by; // Field "CreatedBy" cannot suppressed
*   CreatedAt     = created_at; // Field "CreatedAt" cannot suppressed

    LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
    LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
}

Behavior Pool: Effect on EML Strutcure

  • Once suppressed, the fields are no longer available in EML operations. For example, in a MODIFY ENTITIES call, suppressed fields do not appear in the FIELDS ( ) selector:
 MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_vd_reqhdr IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY RequestHeader
    UPDATE FIELDS (  ) 
* i try add the fields which i have done suppressed those not visible here 
    WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_key IN keys
                  ( %key = ls_key-%key
                  Status = 100 ) )
    REPORTED DATA(lt_report).

  • This confirms that suppressed fields are cleanly removed from the generated TYPE STRUCTURE FOR UPDATE and equivalent EML types.

How Does It Differ from @ui.hidden and @Consumption.hidden?

All three mechanisms can "hide" a field, but they operate at different layers:

Mechanism Layer Effect
field(suppress)RAP behavior / EMLRemoves field from RAP transactional APIs and EML structures
@ui.hidden: trueUI renderingHides the field from generated Fiori screens; field may still appear in OData metadata and API payloads
@Consumption.hidden: trueOData consumptionHides the field from OData consumers and Fiori Elements scenarios
  • Use field(suppress) to protect a field from the RAP behavior layer entirely.
  • Use @ui.hidden to keep a field out of the UI while it remains accessible in APIs.
  • Use @Consumption.hidden to hide a field from external OData consumers.

Conclusion

field(suppress), @ui.hidden, and @Consumption.hidden serve distinct purposes in the RAP stack and should not be treated as interchangeable. When you have internal technical fields — timestamps, UUIDs, control fields — that RAP should never expose through its transactional model, field(suppress) is the right tool. Pair it with @Consumption.hidden to lock down the field at both the behavior and OData layers.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Public Edition, Bank Communication Management
entry.content:
Hi Treasury,

Short answer: The Rule IDs in the matchcode are SAP-delivered standard BCM grouping rules — they are not customer-maintainable in Public Cloud, and the rule that lands on the batch is determined automatically by the grouping engine (by payment method + priority), not forced by the assignment you made in the "Assign Approval Rules for Bank Communication Management" activity.

Where are the rules in the Rule ID matchcode (6_PM_&, 6_PM_0 … 6_PM_6) maintained?

They are not maintained by you — they are delivered by SAP.

In Public Cloud there is no customer activity equivalent to the on-premise FSCM → BCM → Payment Grouping → Rule Maintenance step, which is where these rules would otherwise be created. That activity is not exposed under clean core — which is exactly why you can't find the maintenance step.

This is consistent with how SAP handles comparable BCM-internal rules in Public Cloud: they are pre-defined and hardcoded and cannot be changed by the user. Reference (analogous principle, posting rules): KBA 3374309.

The activity you are in — Assign Approval Rules for Bank Communication Management — is the Public Cloud counterpart of the APM on-premise step Define Rules for Bank Communication Management (FSCM → Advanced Payment Management → Payment Processing → External Interfaces → BCM). Its job is only to map an existing BCM Rule ID to a key combination (order type / company code / house bank / account ID) for the APM→BCM workflow handover — not to create the rule. Reference: SAP S/4HANA APM + MBC blog.

Summary

The matchcode rules are SAP-delivered and cannot be maintained in Public Cloud, so there is no missing step to find. The batch correctly shows the delivered grouping rule that matches its payment method by priority, not the one you assigned. Verify the payment method → rule/priority match, and if you need the determination logic confirmed or a specific rule applied, raise an SAP CASE since the rules are not in your control.

Hope it helps,

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: SAP Developer News for June 25th, 2026
entry.content:

Upcoming Events

0:00 Intro

0:10 Upcoming Events

1:40 SAP Open Source Webinar on the NeoNephos Foundation

2:38 ABAPConf Recordings

3:45 DYK GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

Transcription

 

00;00;00;00 - 00;00;06;00
Announcer
This is the SAP Developer News for June the 25th, 2026.

00;00;06;02 - 00;00;09;24
Thomas Jung
the Developer Advocates are always on the move and evidently almost never

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;26;23
Thomas Jung
at home. Over the next couple of weeks, besides our normal schedule of CodeJam, we got a couple of special events coming up and just a little over a week many of us are going to be at. We are Developers in Berlin. Now, this isn’t an SAP.

00;00;26;25 - 00;00;53;24
Thomas Jung
This is not a specific SAP event, but one just a general event for developers from all around the world. I'll be there. Witalij and Shilpa and Sheena will also be there. So if you happen to find yourself at this event, stop by, say hi, maybe attend one of our sessions. I'm giving a session on Friday about how AI is affecting Developer Relations, Developer Advocacy.

00;00;53;24 - 00;01;25;04
Thomas Jung
And maybe I'll tell you my story about how an AI coding agent decided it was its role to lie to me. It should be a fun story. But then the week after that, many of us will be at CodeConnect and near the SAP main campus. Over in St Leon/ROT, we have HANACon, UI5Con and reCAP, as well as some CodeJams that week, and a great many of the Developer Advocates will be in town to support those events.

00;01;25;06 - 00;01;41;18
Thomas Jung
Speak at them, run the CodeJams, so we look forward to seeing many of you at CodeConnect. So busy couple of weeks as we kick off the summertime in the Northern Hemisphere here, and we hope to see you at an event soon.

00;01;41;21 - 00;02;15;21
DJ Adams
Coming up next month on Friday the 10th of July, there's another SAP Open Source webinar. It's open to all. And the topic is the Neo Foundation Europe's Cloud Sovereignty in Motion. The foundation was established in 2025 as a Linux Foundation Europe initiative, and its core objectives are to strengthen European tech sovereignty, improve interoperability standards, and to enable integrated cloud to edge solutions.

00;02;15;23 - 00;02;34;25
DJ Adams
In this webinar hosted by SAP's Open source Program Office, you can learn how the foundation has evolved, how it's governed, and what it offers, a link to more details, and how and where to register is in the description.

00;02;34;27 - 00;03;06;05
Thomas Jung
This week we saw the ABAPConf online and in-person hybrid event take place, and if you miss it, there are plenty of recordings available on YouTube. In fact, both live streams from the whole day. Track one. Track two are available on YouTube, so something like nearly 18 hours worth of content 18 hours plus we saw some great sessions there by some of our ABAP PM and development colleagues.

00;03;06;08 - 00;03;37;21
Thomas Jung
You know, kicking off the day, talking about the future of ABAP Cloud, about the RAP development model, about ABAP Development Tools now available in Visual Studio Code, and what we're doing with AI tooling there. And at the end of the day, we saw a special presentation by Karl Kessler as he looked back at the history of ABAP. And so all that great content is available on the ABAPConf page on YouTube.

00;03;37;21 - 00;03;46;22
Thomas Jung
And if you're interested in ABAP definitely suggest you check it out.

00;03;46;25 - 00;04;18;06
Rekha D R
Did you know you can enable and use generative AI feature with an SAP Build Process Automation? For that, click on Automated Process Project, provide the name and create the project. Then create a process within it. Switch to the overview page, then click on generate button. Now you have the options to create process, form and data type. When you open the process and click generate button, you can explain this process.

00;04;18;09 - 00;04;36;06
Rekha D R
You can create a business form. For example, a sales order form and AI will intelligently generate the entire form with relevant input fields within the decision artifact. You can summarize the decision. Also have the option to generate new rules with your natural language.

entry.title: Re: Request for Metrics and Threshold Details – SAP Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi 1-Jul,
I appears that part of your question is not available, so I am not sure of the full intent of it.
In the SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring app, metrics can be :

  • Numeric KPIs that evaluate against warning/critical thresholds (for example, memory utilization, CPU, queue lengths).
  • Up/Down or Reachable/Not Reachable status of services or components.
  • Discrete occurrences (for example, certificate expiring soon, failed checks, or system-generated alerts).
  • Events can also be custom-defined.

Recommended threshold values

  • SAP ships default thresholds for many metrics. These defaults are visible in the Health Monitoring app for each managed service/system and can be customized.
  • “Recommended” values depend on system size, workload pattern, and solution type (for example, SAP HANA, ABAP platform, Integration Suite, etc.). SAP’s default thresholds serve as the baseline and are the recommended starting point.

How to locate and share the exact metric lists and defaults

  • In SAP Cloud ALM, built-in help has a wealth of information:
    • Go to Health Monitoring
    • Open the specific managed service/system
    • Navigate to Metrics
    • Each metric shows its current default threshold (warning/critical) and type along with a description
      See also KBA 3613788 - Where to find documentation on what Ok, Warning, Critical and Fatal means in regards to percentages within Health Monitoring? - Cloud ALM
  • On Help Portal:
    • Health Monitoring - Content -> breaks down by Product the various metric categories and metrics
    • To find what metrics are available in a use case: 
      1. The SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal has sections for each Operations use case (e.g. Health Monitoring).
      2. Access the "Content" page for a use case.
      3. The supported metrics on a per-product basis will be listed on the page.
      4. Some pages have a filterable table, where you can type the name of the specific SAP Product you are interested and see the supported metrics for only that product. 

Hopefully the above info is helpful in answering your question.
Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Building a Cashflow for Compass: An approach to NPV calculations for appraisals in SAC (1/3)
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud Compass tool has made it possible to explore how factors interact to influence a KPI using a Monte Carlo simulation. Putting it to work for a common finance use case like an NPV calculation requires a specific approach to modelling your problem and until 2026 QRC1 (Q1), simulations were limited to drivers that SUM over time, like unit counts and total costs. If your model needed drivers for rate-type assumptions like cost per m², rental rates or interest, those elements had to stay fixed, and it was a barrier to moving from an Excel-led approach.

The Q1 release changes that, making average-based drivers* available. If you have an appraisal you re-run regularly, I think Compass is reaching a point where it's worth exploring. It lets you stress-test the elements outside the organisation's control and compare scenarios for the KPIs that are within it .

*rate-type KPIs need average exception aggregation — AVG excl. NULL and AVG incl. unbooked, new in QRC1. New to exception aggregation? See Alecsandra's post.

Part 1 - How to carry balances forward in SAC (so you can stress-test an NPV in Compass)
A cashflow model isn't a row of independent periods - each period's closing balance for cash is next period's opening balance. This also applies to balance sheet accounts - whether that's retained earnings, loan balance, cumulative cash, the discount factor itself: all need the prior period carried forward. In Excel where the data is reduced to a two dimensional representation this is trivial

=E5+D4

You don't have to consider a multidimensional data model, it's as simple as referencing the previous closing balance and adding the current periods movements. The difficulty of moving a cashflow to SAC is that there's no "cell to the left." A measure is defined once and applies to every period at once.

Compass
When using Compass this becomes key as the model re-runs each measure calculation thousands of times with different driver values. Whatever carry-forward mechanism you use has to re-evaluate correctly inside each simulation. 

So what are the options?

  • Time offset / lookup in a calculated measure - This was my first approach - just like I would in excel I simply setup a formula to reference the prior period directly. SAC has both LOOKUP and RESTRICT formula that with a dynamic time filter will return the previous period. This works fine in a story as the DATE is specified in the story. This means it can show a period-period comparison. However, in the compass simulation you aren't looking at it from a single point in time and so these formula's can't drive a recursive carry forward in a compass simulation. 
  • Advanced formula data action - When I've created a cashflow previously to avoid the issues with LOOKUP and RESTRICT I used advanced formulas. Within advanced formulas RESULTLOOKUP can be used to carry forward a balance. When you execute a data action in a story it triggers a write back of the value into the model. At the moment data actions aren't compatible with Compass and so although they would normally be a go to answer for creating cashflows in SAC for this kind of planning scenario they can't be triggered by the simulation and so don't work. 
  • ITERATE - ITERATE builds a running total across periods, by referencing its own result from the period before. Once it's created in the modeller it applies to every period and crucially it works with Compass. As the calculation lives in the model and you can use the calculation tools to check the results. There's a key choice to be aware of: you can use ITERATE on measures or on accounts. Depending on how complex the cash flow is you may need to include other financial statements including a P&L and balance sheet. If you do need these then using accounts lets SAC handle the income, expense, asset and liability behaviour for you - instead of you having to build that yourself. So accounts are a practical choice for a cashflow.

Redesign - When you start to implement ITERATE you will likely find that it doesn't work with your excel cashflow. That's because many cashflows include circular references by design as they are working to solve the cashflow. When you use Compass you may need to rethink that because the simulation has a different purpose. It exists to show you the likelihood of an outcome; you run the model and measure the spread of the NPV. Circular references do the opposite - they use a loop to find the NPV.

Where this is particularly likely to occur is with a loan balance - in this situation the interest depends on the balance and the balance depends on the funding. Rather than solving the funding Compass forces a separation of the appraisal from the funding. This redesign is really good discipline. By keeping the appraisal and funding decisions separated Compass supports more informed decision making.

ITERATE is currently the approach to rolling a balance forward in Compass but it requires a clear understanding of what you are trying to achieve. Part 2 will look at the challenges of using a calculation that references its own result, the places where it doesn't behave in the way I expected and how I worked around it.

entry.title: Joule is Not Magic - Here is the Architecture Behind It
entry.content:
SAP Community already has a great beginner series on Joule. This post is for architects and consultants who already know what Joule is and want to understand how it actually works under the hood.

Before going further, a quick scope note. This post focuses on Joule as an end-user AI copilot embedded within SAP business applications such as S/4HANA, SuccessFactors and Ariba. SAP has since expanded the Joule portfolio to include Joule Studio, Joule for Developers and Joule for Consultants, among others. The grounding architecture described here is most directly relevant to Joule's role as a business copilot, though many of the underlying mechanisms apply across the portfolio.

What is Grounding and Why Does it Matter?

Without grounding, an AI answers questions based on its general training data. The responses may sound fluent and confident but have nothing to do with your organisation's actual data, processes or documents.

Grounding connects the AI to your specific business context. It combines generative LLMs with information retrieval techniques to improve response quality and accuracy, without the complexity and cost of fine-tuning a model on company-specific data. The result is responses that are relevant, accurate and actionable.

This is not a nice to have. It is the difference between a generic chatbot and an enterprise grade AI copilot.

How Joule is Grounded

Joule does not rely on a single grounding mechanism. It combines multiple approaches, each suited to a different type of data and scenario. 

SAP Help Documentation

Joule retrieves information from SAP's official Help Portal and documentation using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). SAP's documentation corpus covers millions of pages across all SAP applications. It is processed using chunking, vectorisation and embedding techniques, then stored in the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine for semantic retrieval.

Ask Joule a procedural question like "How do I create a purchase order in Ariba?" and it retrieves the most relevant documentation chunks and generates a contextual answer grounded in SAP's official content. This layer is managed entirely by SAP. Customers do not configure it.

SAP Domain Models

SAP Domain Models are AI models trained specifically on SAP domain knowledge - including code, data, metadata, business processes, architecture knowledge and documentation. When a user initiates an enquiry or wishes to create code, these models are designed to provide results firmly grounded in the SAP context rather than relying on generic internet knowledge.

Combined with context graphs and agents, SAP Domain Models bring deep SAP knowledge directly to Joule, Joule Agents and SAP applications. This is a distinct grounding layer that complements documentation retrieval - while SAP Help Documentation retrieves relevant content at query time, SAP Domain Models carry SAP knowledge baked into the model itself.
https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-models/domain-models.html

Document Grounding

For organisations that need Joule to answer questions based on internal knowledge, SAP AI Core provides document grounding capabilities. Think SOPs, process guides, compliance policies, onboarding manuals.

The process works as follows. Documents are retrieved from connected enterprise content repositories, chunked and embedded into vector representations, then stored in the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine. When a user asks a question, a similarity search retrieves the most relevant chunks, which are then injected into Joule's context to generate a grounded response.

Out of the box, Microsoft SharePoint is the primary supported connector for Joule's built-in shared document grounding service. Supported file formats include PDF, Word, HTML and common image formats. Content is refreshed daily.

Organisations that need broader connectivity can use SAP AI Core's private document grounding pattern, which supports additional repositories including SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Document Management Service, Google Drive, AWS S3, SFTP and ServiceNow.
https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/generative-ai/grounding-035c455a5a424697b60f4a24b6d791fe

SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) provides the unified data foundation - federating and harmonising data from S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, Ariba and other SAP and non-SAP applications into managed data products. These data products are made available directly on the SAP Generative AI Hub, which grounds the LLM in trusted organisational data. This is the architectural link between BDC and Joule: BDC data products flow into the Generative AI Hub, which uses them to inform Joule's responses.

But data alone is not enough. Joule also needs to understand the meaning and relationships between business objects across systems. An Employee in SuccessFactors and a Cost Center Owner in S/4HANA are the same person. A Vendor in Ariba and a Supplier in S/4HANA Finance refer to the same entity.

This is where SAP Knowledge Graph comes in. Working alongside BDC, it provides fully managed ontologies that help Joule understand processes, relationships and context across the enterprise. Because Joule uses SAP BDC data products and the SAP Knowledge Graph, it can use fully managed ontologies to understand processes, relationships and context across the enterprise, enabling more comprehensive and accurate insight.

BDC and Knowledge Graph are distinct but work together. BDC provides the data. Knowledge Graph provides the semantic understanding of what that data means and how it relates.

Underpinning all of this is SAP HANA Cloud, the database at the core of BDC. It brings together vectors, graphs, relational and spatial data with business context in a single in-memory database, helping to ensure reliable agent reasoning.

Direct API and OData Integration

For scenarios requiring live transactional data, Joule can invoke SAP application APIs directly rather than relying on replicated analytical data.

What is worth highlighting here is that Joule does not just read data. It can act on it. For example, Creating a purchase order, approving a workflow item, triggering an automation via SAP Build Process Automation - all through natural language. This is where Joule moves from being an informational copilot to an operational AI agent.

How it All Fits Together

The foundation for everything described above is the SAP Business AI Platform - the infrastructure layer that brings together SAP AI Core, the Generative AI Hub, BDC data products and the grounding pipeline. Before any grounding mechanism is invoked, Joule performs intent detection and orchestration within this platform, routing queries to the appropriate grounding source. 

User Question
      ↓
Intent Detection and Orchestration
(SAP Generative AI Hub on SAP Business AI Platform)
      ↓
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SAP Help Documentation (HANA Cloud Vector Engine)              │
│ SAP Domain Models (SAP-trained, context graph grounded)        │
│ Document Grounding (SharePoint / AI Core connectors)           │
│ SAP Business Data Cloud data products                          │
│ SAP Knowledge Graph (ontology and semantic layer)          │
│ SAP HANA Cloud (vectors, graphs, relational, spatial)      │
│ Direct API → SAP Applications (read and write)                 │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
      ↓
LLM reasoning over grounded context
      ↓
Response or Action

What This Means for Your AI Roadmap

Organisations planning a Joule rollout should assess their grounding readiness before going live, not after.

SAP Help documentation grounding and SAP Domain Models require nothing from the customer - SAP manages them. For the remaining layers, there are real questions to answer. Where does your internal knowledge live, and is SharePoint available or do you need a private grounding setup? Is SAP Business Data Cloud part of your landscape - if not, the cross-system grounding will be limited. Are you tracking the maturity of SAP Knowledge Graph for your specific applications? And for agentic scenarios, what authorisation and governance controls need to be in place before Joule starts executing business actions?

These are not technical edge cases. They are architecture decisions that directly determine how useful Joule will be in practice.

entry.title: Extending the Value of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Beyond Salary Reviews
entry.content:
One common misconception about the SAP SuccessFactors Compensation module is that it can only be used for the salary review process.  While that remains its most common and leading practice use case, the module can also be used to support a broader range of adjacent HR and reward processes.

This post explores three practical ways organisations can extend the value of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation beyond the standard salary review use case.

 
Compensation Statement Generation

At the end of a compensation review cycle, forms are moved into the completion step and compensation statements are generated for employees.  In some organisations, however, the end to end salary review process is fixed, centrally determined, or applied directly in Employee Central.  In these scenarios, the Compensation module can still be used to support statement generation.

To configure a statement only template, pull the updated salaries after the fixed increase is applied in Employee Central or apply a fixed increase directly to all eligible employees in the worksheet.  Configure a one step centralised workflow, or a read only simplified workflow for business users to review outcomes, before routing worksheets to completion.  Once worksheets are completed, statements can be generated as usual based on the fixed increases.

This modified process allows organisations to generate compensation statements without running a full manager-input review cycle.  The approach takes advantage of the automated letter distribution, employee and manager statement notifications, and self service access, which are core features of the module.

 
Data Validation

Many HR and reward processes depend on accurate employee data.  When the ownership of employee data belongs to the business, Compensation worksheets and route maps can provide a structured way to support the validation activities.

A read-only form can display Employee Central data as at a defined point in time, giving your business data owners a consolidated view of the employees they are responsible for.  Instead of relying on static reporting, a workflow can be used to assign multiple reviewers and push accountability for the validity of data back to the business.  While changes or corrections to the data must take place in Employee Central, this creative use case of a compensation form template can form part of your salary review activities.

 
Simple Bonus Calculations

Variable Pay is often the preferred solution for incentive payments but if your organisation has a simple point-in-time bonus that does not require complex employee historical information, the Compensation module can be a practical alternative.  Gainshare or simple profit distribution plans are examples that often fall into this category.  In these cases, the standard lump sum field can be used to calculate a bonus payment without impacting an employee’s fixed salary component.

 

These creative solutions are just a few of the examples in which customers can use the functionality of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation for more than just a once a year review tool.  I’d love to hear in the comments other ways in which your organisation or client has used the module for creative business requirements.

entry.title: Re: Agent builder in Joule Studio is now generally available: Build your own Joule agents today!
entry.content: Great to see agent builder hit GA. For shops still on ECC or heavily customized S/4  where Joule Studio's reach is thinnest, we've had good results running a model-agnostic agent layer over OData/RFC-BAPI with governed write-back

entry.title: Re: ECP Custom Development
entry.content: please have a look into our Service Catalogue: https://help.sap.com/doc/service-catalog-for-employee-central-payroll-based-on-sap-human-capital-management-for-sap-erp-hcm/2505/en-US/Service_Catalog_V2.9.zip Customer are allowed: Customer can develop new objects in defined areas (NO modification of existing objects allowed! "Customer can develop new objects in defined areas - no interference with SAP standard, no impact on sizing and performance. Allowed areas are: new payroll functions, payroll operations, new custom tables, new custom infotypes, new fields for SAP-defined infotypes with customer-include, new reports, BADI implementations. Anything that could raise conflicts during upgrade or patch is not allowed. This means also using ""implicit enhancement points"" or applying notes that include modifications that are not part of a future Support package (such notes are for On Premise and must not be applied in the hosted payroll system). The development rights assigned to a customer may theoretically allow the customer to circumvent the aforementioned restrictions. Should SAP determine that modifications are made outside of the allowed areas mentioned above, SAP cannot be held responsible for adhering to the agreed SLA for EC Payroll. In such cases, the customer is responsible for all such modifications and their consequences."

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | APAC
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to the below online webinar where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: RFZALI20 schließt keine stornierten Belege aus
entry.content:
Hallo,
die Zahlungsliste (RFZALI20) muss alle Zahlungen in einem Zahlungslauf oder in einem Zahlungsträgermedienlauf melden (ohne den Parameter „Zahlungsbelegprüfung“ eines anderen Programms zu berücksichtigen). Daher finden Sie in dieser Zahlungsliste auch das stornierte Dokument.
Wenn das Feld 'Zahlungsbeleg-Verprobung' in der relevanten Variante für das Format gesetzt ist, wird der stornierte Belg nicht auf dem DTA ausgegeben, siehe bitte F1-Hilfe zum Feld:": Wenn Sie dieses Kennzeichen setzen, führt das System eine Zahlung nur dann aus, wenn der zugrundeliegende Beleg vorhanden und noch gültig ist.."
Die Liste der Zahlungen in der Datei finden Sie in FDTA ( Shift+F4 Zahlungssliste). Bitte überprüfen Sie diese Liste.

The payment list (RFZALI20) has to report all payments in a payment run or in a payment media run (not taking the parameter "Payment Document Validation" of another program into account). Therefore you will also find the reversed document in this payment list.
The list of payments in the file can be found in FDTA (see last screenshot in my attachment, button Shift+F4 Payment Summary). Please review this list.

Viele Grüße
,Mónika

entry.title: How to display Medium or Long Text instead of 15-character Short Text in SAP GR (FS Item)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'd like to ask is it possible to replace FS Item Short Text with Medium/Long Text to avoid text truncation in Group Reporting?

Since our FS Item descriptions are maintained in English, many of them:
  • exceed 15 characters
  • get truncated in reports and apps
This affects readability for end users.
Thank you in advance for your advice!

entry.title: Re: Improved Data Action scoping with the new Data Filter Parameter type
entry.content:
Nice addition! This would be even more useful if the selected date range from the story filter will be passed automatically as a parameter to the Data Action.

Currently, when a user selects or changes the date in the story filter, they must manually select the same date range again when executing the Data Action. Automatically passing the story filter's date selection to the Data Action parameters would streamline the process, reduce manual effort, and help prevent inconsistencies between the story filter and the Data Action.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports Developer Version 14-04-0-5335
entry.content:
How are you connecting to the XLXS files?

You can enable CR's database logging component to see what is happening:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1603398

Look for the string length, CR is querying the Client to get the length, it's likely returning 65k or more, you'll have to go to makers to fix it.

I don't see xlxs as a data source in CR Database wizard:

So likely cause is the Excel client you are using is returning the length, likely 65K.

A quick Google search for this, see if MS's client works for you:

1. Install the Drivers [1]
  • Most Windows systems require the installation of the Microsoft Access Database Engine Redistributable. This package installs the necessary 32-bit or 64-bit OLEDB and ODBC drivers for modern Office extensions (.xlsx). [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
 
2. Configure the ODBC Data Source (DSN) [1]
  1. Open the Windows Start menu and search for ODBC Data Sources.
  2. Choose 64-bit or 32-bit to strictly match the architecture of the application reading the data.
  3. Under the User DSN or System DSN tab, click Add....
  4. Select Microsoft Excel Driver (*.xls, *.xlsx, *.xlsm, *.xlsb) and click Finish.
  5. Provide a memorable Data Source Name (DSN).
  6. Click Select Workbook..., navigate to your target .xlsx file, and confirm. [1, 2, 3, 4]

Don

entry.title: Re: Understanding field(suppress) in SAP RAP and How It Differs from @UI.hidden and @Consumption.hid
entry.content: Your explanation of how field (suppress) works at the RAP behavior layer is extremely clear and practical. I especially liked the comparison with @ui.hidden and readonly, and the runtime observations you shared about EML structures and draft handling. This really helps clarify when suppression is the safer option for protecting audit fields in RAP-based applications.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic height control of Tabstrip in classic Dynpro — is it possible?
entry.content: Dynpro can’t resize tabstrips dynamically. The usual workaround is to keep the container fixed and just load different subscreens based on the tab. If you truly need dynamic resizing, you’d have to move to control framework or a modern UI.

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Hi @dawid90,

I get product master data using RETAIL_ST_PL_PRODUCT_ENH badi and assign value to custom field (this field is added via Custom Fields app). This field is also exposed to RETAILSTORE_PRODUCT_LKP_SRV services.

If I want to bind this field value in UI, how to bind this? I added like this in xml file but it doesn't work. Is there any reference syntax for this?

Logic in RETAIL_ST_PL_PRODUCT_ENH badi

METHOD if_retail_st_pl_product_enh~get_product_supp_data.

DATA lv_product_gp TYPE string.

IF ct_product-matnr IS NOT INITIAL.

SELECT SINGLE ProductGroup

FROM I_Product with privileged access

WHERE Product = @ct_product-matnr

INTO @lv_product_gp.

ct_product-yy1_productdescriptio2_lkp = lv_product_gp.

ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

XML code in BAS

 

Thank You.

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: 【SAP For Me/S User】SAPからの通知メール内「EVBxxxxxxxx」の内容詳細を表示する権限の不足
entry.content:
【SAP For Me/S User】SAPからの通知メール内「EVBxxxxxxxx」の内容詳細を表示する権限の不足

対象環境: CLOUD / cba3f6a9-29e5-440d-8c77-c910fb68b626 / Production

受信メール件名:　SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime — Deprecation and Upcoming Removal of TLS 1.2 Ciphers

内容詳細:

特定のSユーザで、SAPから受信した上記通知メール内リンク「EVBxxxxxxxx」へアクセスし、
SAP For Me ->Systems & Provisioning ->EVBxxxxxxxx　の詳細を確認しようとしたところ
権限不足により「制限付きカードコンテンツ」と表示され、閲覧が出来ない状態となっております。

上記画面内「権限依頼」を押下/又は対象カードの権限詳細情報を確認することで
「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」権限が必要とありますが、どちらかのみで問題ないでしょうか。
またはどちらの権限も付与が必要でしょうか。
他に付与が必要な権限がありましたらご教示ください。
参照ページ: About the User, Authorization and Administrator Concept 
　　　　　DIGITS - 特定のカードを使用するために必要な S ユーザ ID 権限の決定方法- SAP for Me - SAP for Me 

また、弊社では「システムデータの表示/編集」権限付与の際は、対象のインストレーションによって管理・許可しているため
対象メールのカードを表示するにあたりインストレーションを適切に付与する必要があります。
本件はSAP BTP関連の通知のため、以下インストレーションが該当すると考えておりますが、正否についてご教示ください。
インストレーション:　DIGITS(SAP CLOUD PLATFORM)

付与対象のSユーザ情報、閲覧不可となっているSAP通知メール等の詳細情報につきましては、ドキュメントにまとめ、後ほどアップロードいたします。

※本ケースの対象コンポーネントが誤っている場合は、お手数ですが適切なコンポーネントへのご転送をお願いいたします。

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 【SAP For Me/S User】SAPからの通知メール内「EVBxxxxxxxx」の内容詳細を表示す...
entry.content:
ご質問について、以下の回答をご確認するようにお願い申し上げます。

1.「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」権限が必要とありますが、どちらかのみで問題ないでしょうか。またはどちらの権限も付与が必要でしょうか。他に付与が必要な権限がありましたらご教示ください。

--->はい、どちらかのみで問題ないです。該当KBA 2587408 - S-user ID authorizations for Cloud Availability and Manage Cloud Service Availability Notifications (Get Notified) - SAP for Meをご確認ください。

該当内容を抜粋致します、ご参考いただければと思います。

In order to see your cloud products and systems for a particular installation, your S-user ID must have one of the following authorizations. They can be set at the cloud installation number level, customer number level, or CCC level.

Display System Data
Edit System Data

つまり、「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」権限をどちらのみで付与すれば問題ないです。

2.本件はSAP BTP関連の通知のため、以下インストレーションが該当すると考えておりますが、正否についてご教示ください。インストレーション:　*

--->はい、インストレーション:　*該当です。

３．作業実行ユーザ：*

--->弊社バックエンドで調べておりますが、そのS-user様は自分がインストレーション *にて該当権限を持っていないです。

ユーザー管理のルール上、管理者は自身に割り当てられた権限のみを管理可能となっております。そのS-user様は当該インストール番号*に対する権限を保有していないため、「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」の権限を操作することができません。当該ユーザーの権限は他のインストール番号に紐付けられています。

*様に権限を付与する必要がある場合は、の当該インストール番号のクラウド管理者（Cloud Admin）までご連絡ください。
